notesByAuthority.frozenProtocol: Frozen protocol execution must not widen evidence lookup beyond the packaged bundle. STOP_AND_BLOCK if stronger support would be required.
notesByAuthority.boundedEvidenceSeeking: Bounded evidence-seeking may refresh current papers, textbooks, standards, and guidelines only through the packaged evidence classes and retrieval routes; strong claims still require corroboration.
notesByAuthority.openEndedBrowsingDefault: Open-ended discovery may widen source lookup beyond the named source inventory, but defaults, versions, deprecations, and strong scientific claims still require official guidance plus corroborating benchmark or literature support.

notesByAuthority.frozenProtocol: No new live source discovery should occur under frozen protocol execution.
notesByAuthority.boundedEvidenceSeeking: Every fetched source must remain inside the packaged evidence classes and approved retrieval-route families.
notesByAuthority.openEndedBrowsingDefault: Named domains and repositories define the packaged discovery floor, not a strict ceiling. Newly discovered sources remain admissible only when their source class, access pattern, freshness, and contradiction handling satisfy this policy.

workbookPolicy.evidenceRole: Workbook curation is admissible as structured internal evidence for candidate nomination and contradiction surfacing, but it is not authoritative relative to official repository metadata or endpoint records.
evidenceNotes: Workbook-derived records may seed candidate datasets, sample mappings, and split hypotheses when provenance is preserved.
Workbook contradictions must remain visible in runtime provenance rather than being flattened into generic metadata warnings.
When workbook curation disagrees with official endpoint metadata, preserve both as competing evidence and fail closed on admission or claim promotion until the conflict is resolved.
